data.title: Bitcoin and ethereum surge to August highs as Trump plans to add cryptos to 401(k) plans in new EO.
data.description: Bitcoin and ethereum have experienced significant increases in value, reaching highs not seen since August. This surge comes as President Trump prepares to reveal an executive order that will include cryptocurrencies in 401(k) retirement plans. The move is expected to integrate digital assets into traditional investment portfolios, potentially impacting a large number of individuals saving for retirement. This development has sparked enthusiasm among investors and analysts, as it signifies a growing acceptance of cryptocurrency within the mainstream financial sector.

data.title: IonQ reported larger Q2 loss than expected, but revenue exceeded views. Analyst downgrades stock due to growing risks.
data.description: Commencing the financial report for quantum computing companies, IonQ unveiled a larger-than-anticipated Q2 loss alongside revenue that exceeded expectations. In light of this, an analyst downgraded IonQ due to escalating risks.

data.title: Eli Lilly stock plunges 14% due to obesity setback, boosting rivals; Investor's Business Daily reports.
data.description: Eli Lilly's stock experienced a significant decline of 14% on Thursday due to an unforeseen setback in its obesity franchise. This led to a surge in the shares of its competitors.

data.title: DraftKings stock surged after earnings, exiting buy zone. Flutter Entertainment reports next. (Chars: 78)
data.description: Following its late Wednesday earnings report, DraftKings stock surged out of a buy zone. Flutter Entertainment is set to report its earnings on Thursday. This development was covered in an article titled "DraftKings Pushes Out Of Buy Zone On Results, FanDuel Parent Up Next" by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: DoorDash stock surges after strong Q2 with 25% sales growth, aiming for record high as food delivery becomes consumer staple.
data.description: DoorDash's stock is poised to continue its upward trend after a successful second quarter, which resulted in a 25% increase in sales for the food-delivery company. The market anticipates reaching a record high as food delivery services become a staple for consumers.

data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery reports $9.81 billion revenue, $1.58 billion net income, and $1.95 billion adjusted EBITDA in Q2.
data.description: Warner Bros. Discovery stock saw a fluctuation after the company's fiscal second-quarter results were released, reporting quarterly revenue of $9.81 billion, flat year-on-year excluding foreign exchange growth. This figure missed the analyst consensus estimate of $9.72 billion. The earnings per share were 63 cents, exceeding the analyst consensus estimate of a 22-cent loss. The company reported a net income of $1.58 billion, a significant improvement from the $9.99 billion loss the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA stood at $1.95 billion, showing a 9% growth excluding foreign exchange. Distribution revenues remained steady at $4.89 billion, while advertising revenues dropped 10% to $2.22 billion due to a decline in domestic linear audiences. Content revenues rose by 16% to $2.47 billion, driven by a stronger performance of theatrical releases. The quarter also saw the generation of $983 million in operating cash flow and $702 million in free cash flow by WBD.
